meta.title: General Intervention Process & Patient Assessment
notes: Swim-lane phases on the chart: Preliminary Assessment (SSS) -> Primary Assessment -> Secondary Assessment -> Continuous Assessment.
Side mnemonics are reference aids transcribed into the relevant node text: SETUP, AVPU, ABCDE, SAMPLER, OPQRST, GCS, PERRLA, Vital signs.
'Need reinforcement?' and 'Determine the number of patients' are scene reminders shown beside the preliminary phase.

gen_01.text: Safety: ensure safety & security for staff, patient(s) and bystanders. [SETUP: Stop (take a few seconds), Environment, Traffic, Unpredictable, PPE (Personal Protective Equipment).]

gen_02.text: Scene Evaluation: mechanism of injury; environmental factors. Determine the number of patients. Need reinforcement?

gen_03.text: Situation Evaluation: first general impression; main complaint.

gen_06.text: Primary Assessment — Look-Listen-Feel-Do. AVPU: Alert & Awake / Verbal response / Pain stimuli response / Unconscious. ABCDE: Airway (open mouth) & Axis; Breathing (look, listen, feel); Circulation (skin colour, capillary refill, pulse quality); Disability (neurologic); Expose & Protect - Evacuate (priority).

gen_07.text: Intervention Strategy — is the patient stable or unstable?

gen_09.text: Unstable: immediate action: BLS. Limit time on site.

gen_10.text: Secondary Assessment — Measure (equipment) - Look-Listen-Feel-Do: medical history; vital signs; pain scale. [SAMPLER: Signs & Symptoms, Allergies, Medication, Past history, Last oral intake, Events, Risk factors. OPQRST: Onset, Provoked by, Quality, Radiation, Severity, Time. Vital signs: B respiration rate + SpO2; C heart rate + blood pressure; D Glasgow Coma Scale + pupils + blood sugar; E temperature. GCS: eye-opening (1-4), verbal (1-5), motor (1-6). PERRLA: Pupils Equal Round Reactive to Light Accommodation.]

gen_11.text: Continuous Assessment & Orientation: reassessment of patient; strategy & treatment; determine the most appropriate care center.
